content:
Your submission is already quite strong, but refining the structure and adding troubleshooting details would take it to the next level. Great job!

Best,
[Your Name]

⸻

Alvin Gonzalez Naranjo – “Creating a Pivot Table in Excel”

Dear Alvin,

Your guide on creating a pivot table for financial tracking is well-structured and informative. You do a great job of breaking down the process into simple steps, which is essential for someone new to using pivot tables. The use of visuals is also a strong feature, as they help reinforce the written instructions.

One area that could be improved is consistency in detail. Some steps, like copying the data, are clearly explained, while others, like setting up the pivot table fields, could use more elaboration. Consider adding brief explanations about why each step is necessary and how users can customize the table to fit their needs.

Your conclusion is encouraging and reinforces the usefulness of pivot tables, but it might be helpful to include a troubleshooting section. What should users do if the pivot table doesn’t update correctly? What if they accidentally delete important data? Addressing common issues would make this even more helpful for first-time users.
context: From document: Workshop-Style Feedback Letters — chunk 4 (chunk 4/7)
